title: ext_type: encoding@1
description: Extension block for reversible codecs (base64, percent-encoding, punycode, ...). The load-bearing field is `test_vectors`: CI actually executes each `input -> output` transform and asserts equality, so codec facts are proven, not asserted. Strings that carry control/non-printable bytes must use JSON escapes (e.g. \u001b), never literal control bytes, consistent with the rest of the corpus.

rfc.description: The defining specification, normalized (e.g. 'RFC4648' for base64/base32/base16, 'RFC3986' for percent-encoding, 'RFC3492' for punycode). Single canonical reference; cross-references go in core see_also / attribution.

charset.description: Optional. The character set / repertoire the codec operates over or targets (e.g. 'US-ASCII', 'UTF-8'). Omitted when not meaningful for the codec.

alphabet.description: Optional. The output alphabet, verbatim, for alphabet-based codecs (e.g. base64's 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/'). Omitted for codecs without a fixed alphabet (e.g. percent-encoding).

algorithm.description: Codec selector consumed by the encoding-exec gate to pick the transform. One of: base64, base64url, base32, base16 (alias hex), percent (aliases percent-encoding/percent_encoding/url), punycode (alias idna). May be overridden per test_vector. Distinct from `rfc`, which is the citable spec id.

test_vectors.description: Executable proof set. CI applies the codec to each `input` (interpreted per `input_form`) and asserts the result equals `output` (interpreted per `output_form`). Round-trip is checked by also decoding back where the codec is reversible. At least one vector is required so every encoding entry is provably correct.

input.description: The input value, encoded per `input_form`. Non-printable bytes use JSON escapes.

input_form.description: How to interpret `input` before applying the codec.

output.description: The expected result, encoded per `output_form`. Non-printable bytes use JSON escapes.

output_form.description: How to interpret `output` for the equality assertion.

algorithm.description: Optional per-vector codec override consumed by the encoding-exec gate (falls back to ext.algorithm). Same enum as ext.algorithm.

direction.description: Which way the codec runs for this vector. 'encode' applies input->output; 'decode' applies the inverse. Default 'encode'.

input_encoding.description: How the gate reads `input` bytes when running the codec (Node Buffer encoding). Default 'utf8'.

note.description: Optional human-readable note (e.g. 'RFC 4648 §10 test vector', 'padding edge case', 'IDNA example from RFC 3492').

notes.description: Optional codec-level gotchas/clarifications (e.g. padding rules, URL-safe variant, IDNA prefix). Free text, one per item.
